Topology management: node registry, groups, and health state.\n2\t\n3\tuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\n4\tuse std::collections::HashMap;\n5\tuse std::fmt;\n6\t\n7\t/// Unique identifier for a node.\n8\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Hash, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n9\tpub struct NodeId(String);\n10\t\n11\timpl NodeId {\n12\t    /// Create a new NodeId.\n13\t    pub fn new(id: String) -> Self {\n14\t        Self(id)\n15\t    }\n16\t\n17\t    /// Get the node ID as a string slice.\n18\t    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &str {\n19\t        &self.0\n20\t    }\n21\t}\n22\t\n23\timpl From<String> for NodeId {\n24\t    fn from(s: String) -> Self {\n25\t        Self(s)\n26\t    }\n27\t}\n28\t\n29\timpl AsRef<str> for NodeId {\n30\t    fn as_ref(&self) -> &str {\n31\t        &self.0\n32\t    }\n33\t}\n34\t\n35\t/// Health status of a node.\n36\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n37\tpub enum NodeStatus {\n38\t    /// Node is healthy and serving traffic.\n39\t    Healthy,\n40\t    /// Node is degraded (intermittent failures, still serving traffic).\n41\t    Degraded,\n42\t    /// Node is active and fully operational (synonym for Healthy).\n43\t    Active,\n44\t    /// Node is joining the cluster (being provisioned).\n45\t    Joining,\n46\t    /// Node is draining (graceful shutdown, not accepting new writes).\n47\t    Draining,\n48\t    /// Node has failed (unplanned outage).\n49\t    Failed,\n50\t    /// Node has been removed from the cluster (tracked for migration).\n51\t    Removed,\n52\t}\n53\t\n54\timpl NodeStatus {\n55\t    /// Check if a transition from `self` to `new_status` is valid.\n56\t    ///\n57\t    /// # State Transition Rules\n58\t    ///\n59\t    /// | From | To | Triggered by |\n60\t    /// |------|-----|-------------|\n61\t    /// | (new) | Joining | `POST /_miroir/nodes` |\n62\t    /// | Joining | Active | Migration complete |\n63\t    /// | Active | Draining | `POST /_miroir/nodes/{id}/drain` |\n64\t    /// | Draining | Removed | Migration complete |\n65\t    /// | Active/Draining | Failed | Health check detects |\n66\t    /// | Failed | Active | Health check recovery |\n67\t    /// | Active/Failed | Degraded | Partial health |\n68\t    /// | Degraded | Active | Health restored |\n69\t    pub fn can_transition_to(self, new_status: NodeStatus) -> bool {\n70\t        match (self, new_status) {\n71\t            // Initial state\n72\t            (NodeStatus::Joining, NodeStatus::Active) => true,\n73\t\n74\t            // Normal operations\n75\t            (NodeStatus::Active, NodeStatus::Draining) => true,\n76\t            (NodeStatus::Draining, NodeStatus::Removed) => true,\n77\t\n78\t            // Failure and recovery\n79\t            (NodeStatus::Active, NodeStatus::Failed) => true,\n80\t            (NodeStatus::Draining, NodeStatus::Failed) => true,\n81\t            (NodeStatus::Failed, NodeStatus::Active) => true,\n82\t\n83\t            // Degradation\n84\t            (NodeStatus::Active, NodeStatus::Degraded) => true,\n85\t            (NodeStatus::Failed, NodeStatus::Degraded) => true,\n86\t            (NodeStatus::Degraded, NodeStatus::Active) => true,\n87\t\n88\t            // Healthy <-> Active are bidirectional (synonyms)\n89\t            (NodeStatus::Healthy, NodeStatus::Active) => true,\n90\t            (NodeStatus::Active, NodeStatus::Healthy) => true,\n91\t\n92\t            // Same state is always valid\n93\t            (s, t) if s == t => true,\n94\t\n95\t            // All other transitions are invalid\n96\t            _ => false,\n97\t        }\n98\t    }\n99\t\n100\t    /// Returns `true` if the node can accept writes for the given shard.\n101\t    ///\n102\t    /// # Write Eligibility Rules\n103\t    ///\n104\t    /// A node is write-eligible for a shard based on its status:\n105\t    ///\n106\t    /// | Status | Write Eligible | Notes |\n107\t    /// |--------|----------------|-------|\n108\t    /// | Healthy/Active | Yes | Normal operation |\n109\t    /// | Degraded | Yes | Partial failures, still accepting writes |\n110\t    /// | Joining | No | Being provisioned, not yet ready |\n111\t    /// | Draining | Conditional | Only for shards it still owns during migration |\n112\t    /// | Failed | No | Unavailable |\n113\t    /// | Removed | No | No longer in cluster |\n114\t    ///\n115\t    /// The `draining_shard` parameter should be `Some(shard_id)` if the node\n116\t    /// is in `Draining` status and the shard IS being actively migrated off this node\n117\t    /// (use `None` if the shard is not being drained or no shard is being checked).\n118\t    /// When `Some(...)`, the node is NOT eligible for writes.\n119\t    pub fn is_write_eligible_for(self, draining_shard: Option<u32>) -> bool {\n120\t        match self {\n121\t            NodeStatus::Healthy | NodeStatus::Active | NodeStatus::Degraded => true,\n122\t            NodeStatus::Joining | NodeStatus::Failed | NodeStatus::Removed => false,\n123\t            NodeStatus::Draining => !draining_shard.is_some(),\n124\t        }\n125\t    }\n126\t}\n127\t\n128\timpl fmt::Display for NodeStatus {\n129\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n130\t        match self {\n131\t            NodeStatus::Healthy => write!(f, \"healthy\"),\n132\t            NodeStatus::Degraded => write!(f, \"degraded\"),\n133\t            NodeStatus::Active => write!(f, \"active\"),\n134\t            NodeStatus::Joining => write!(f, \"joining\"),\n135\t            NodeStatus::Draining => write!(f, \"draining\"),\n136\t            NodeStatus::Failed => write!(f, \"failed\"),\n137\t            NodeStatus::Removed => write!(f, \"removed\"),\n138\t        }\n139\t    }\n140\t}\n141\t\n142\t/// A single Meilisearch node in the topology.\n143\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n144\tpub struct Node {\n145\t    /// Unique node identifier.\n146\t    pub id: NodeId,\n147\t\n148\t    /// Node base URL / address.\n149\t    pub address: String,\n150\t\n151\t    /// Current health status.\n152\t    pub status: NodeStatus,\n153\t\n154\t    /// Replica group assignment (0-based).\n155\t    pub replica_group: u32,\n156\t}\n157\t\n158\timpl Node {\n159\t    /// Create a new node.\n160\t    pub fn new(id: NodeId, address: String, replica_group: u32) -> Self {\n161\t        Self {\n162\t            id,\n163\t            address,\n164\t            status: NodeStatus::Joining,\n165\t            replica_group,\n166\t        }\n167\t    }\n168\t\n169\t    /// Create a new node with a specific status.\n170\t    pub fn with_status(id: NodeId, address: String, replica_group: u32, status:  /// Transition the node to a new status, validating the transition.\n185\t    ///\n186\t    /// Returns `Ok(())` if the transition is valid, `Err` otherwise.\n187\t    pub fn set_status(&mut self, new_status: NodeStatus) -> Result<(), TransitionError> {\n188\t        if self.status.can_transition_to(new_status) {\n189\t            self.status = new_status;\n190\t            Ok(())\n191\t        } else {\n192\t            Err(TransitionError {\n193\t                from: self.status,\n194\t                to: new_status,\n195\t            })\n196\t        }\n197\t    }\n198\t\n199\t    /// migrated off this node. The caller should pass\n203\t    /// `Some(shard_id)` if the shard is being drained from this node.\n204\t    pub fn is_write_eligible_for(&self, shard_id: Option<u32>) -> bool {\n205\t }\n224\t}\n225\t\n226\timpl std::error::Error for TransitionError {}\n227\t\n228\t/// A replica group: an independent query pool.\n229\t///\n230\t/// Each group holds all S shards, distributed across pub id: u32,\n236\t\n237\t    /// Nodes in this group.\n238\t    nodes: Vec<NodeId>,\n239\t}\n240\t\n241\timpl Group {\n242\t    /// Create a new group.\n243\t    pub fn new(id: u32) -> Self {\n244\t        Self {\n245\t            id,\n246\t            nodes: Vec::new(),\n247\t        }\n248\t    }\n249\t\n250\t    /// Add a node to this group.\n251\t    pub fn add_node(&mut self, node_id: NodeId) self.nodes.len()\n265\t    }\n266\t\n267\t    /// Get all healthy nodes in this group, looking them up from the topology.\n268\t    ///\n269\t    /// This requires access to the topology's node map to .filter_map(|node_id| all_nodes.get(node_id))\n274\t            .filter(|node| node.is_healthy())\n275\t            .collect()\n276\t    }\n277\t}\n278\t\n279\t/// Cluster topology: groups, nodes, and add_node(&mut self, node: Node) {\n308\t        let group_id = node.replica_group as usize;\n309\t\n310\t        // Ensure group exists\n311\t        while self.groups.len() <= group_id {\n312\t            self.groups.push(Group::new(self.groups.len() as u32));\n313\t        }\n314\t\n315\t        self.groups[group_id].add_node(node.id.clone());\n316\t        self.nodes.insert(node.id.clone(), assert!(!node.is_healthy());\n403\t\n404\t        // Removed status is not healthy\n405\t        node.status = NodeStatus::Removed;\n406\t        assert!(!node.is_healthy());\n407\t    }\n408\t\n409\t    #[test]\n410\t    fn test_group_node_count() {\n411\t        let mut group = Group::new(0);\n412\t        assert_eq!(group.node_count(), 0);\n413\t\n414\t        group.add_node(NodeId::new(\"node1\".to_string()));\n415\t        assert_eq!(group.node_count(), 1);\n416\t\n417\t        group.add_node(NodeId::new(\"node2\".to_string()));\n418\t        assert_eq!(group.node_count(), 2);\n419\t\n420\t        // Adding duplicate node doesn't increase count\n421\t        group.add_node(NodeId::new(\"node1\".to_string()));\n422\t        assert_eq!(group.node_count(), 2);\n423\t    }\n424\t\n425\t    #[test]\n426\t    fn test_topology_replica_group_count() {\n427\t        let mut topology = Topology::new(64, 2);\n428\t\n429\t assert_eq!(NodeStatus::Failed.to_string(), \"failed\");\n806\t        assert_eq!(NodeStatus::Removed.to_string(), \"removed\");\n807\t    }\n808\t\n809\t    #[test]\n810\t    fn assert!(msg.contains(\"draining\"));\n819\t    }\n820\t}\n821\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"21ce79bf-f310-4f20-b27d-8a24346682c8","uuid":"fb35e9fc-11e1-4d17-ad2c-98213803ddf2","timestamp":"2026-05-09T19:08:34.970Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-core/src/topology.rs","content":"//! Topology management: node registry, groups, and health state.\n\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::fmt;\n\n/// Unique identifier for a node.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Hash, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct NodeId(String);\n\nimpl NodeId {\n    /// Create a new NodeId.\n    pub fn new(id: String) -> Self {\n        Self(id)\n    }\n\n    /// Get the node ID as a string slice.\n    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &str {\n        &self.0\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From<String> for NodeId {\n    fn from(s: String) -> Self {\n        Self(s)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl AsRef<str> for NodeId {\n    fn as_ref(&self) -> &str {\n        &self.0\n    }\n}\n\n/// Health status of a node.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub enum NodeStatus {\n    /// Node is healthy and serving traffic.\n    Healthy,\n    /// Node is degraded (intermittent failures, still serving traffic).\n    Degraded,\n    /// Node is active and fully operational (synonym for Healthy).\n    Active,\n    /// Node is joining the cluster (being provisioned).\n    Joining,\n    /// Node is draining (graceful shutdown, not accepting new writes).\n    Draining,\n    /// Node has failed (unplanned outage).\n    Failed,\n    /// Node has been removed from the cluster (tracked for migration).\n    Removed,\n}\n\nimpl NodeStatus {\n    /// Check if a transition from `self` to `new_status` is valid.\n    ///\n    /// # State Transition Rules\n    ///\n    /// | From | To | Triggered by |\n    /// |------|-----|-------------|\n    /// | (new) | Joining | `POST /_miroir/nodes` |\n    /// | Joining | Active | Migration complete |\n    /// | Active | Draining | `POST /_miroir/nodes/{id}/drain` |\n    /// | Draining | Removed | Migration complete |\n    /// | Active/Draining | Failed | Health check detects |\n    /// | Failed | Active | Health check recovery |\n    /// | Active/Failed | Degraded | Partial health |\n    /// | Degraded | Active | Health restored |\n    pub fn can_transition_to(self, new_status: NodeStatus) -> bool {\n        match (self, new_status) {\n            // Initial state\n            (NodeStatus::Joining, NodeStatus::Active) => true,\n\n            // Normal operations\n            (NodeStatus::Active, NodeStatus::Draining) => true,\n            (NodeStatus::Draining, NodeStatus::Removed) => true,\n\n            // Failure and recovery\n            (NodeStatus::Active, NodeStatus::Failed) => true,\n            (NodeStatus::Draining, NodeStatus::Failed) => true,\n            (NodeStatus::Failed, NodeStatus::Active) => true,\n\n            // Degradation\n            (NodeStatus::Active, NodeStatus::Degraded) => true,\n            (NodeStatus::Failed, NodeStatus::Degraded) => true,\n            (NodeStatus::Degraded, NodeStatus::Active) => true,\n\n            // Healthy <-> Active are bidirectional (synonyms)\n            (NodeStatus::Healthy, NodeStatus::Active) => true,\n            (NodeStatus::Active, NodeStatus::Healthy) => true,\n\n            // Same state is always valid\n            (s, t) if s == t => true,\n\n            // All other transitions are invalid\n            _ => false,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Returns `true` if the node can accept writes for the given shard.\n    ///\n    /// # Write Eligibility Rules\n    ///\n    /// A node is write-eligible for a shard based on its status:\n    ///\n    /// | Status | Write Eligible | Notes |\n    /// |--------|----------------|-------|\n    /// | Healthy/Active | Yes | Normal operation |\n    /// | Degraded | Yes | Partial failures, still accepting writes |\n    /// | Joining | No | Being provisioned, not yet ready |\n    /// | Draining | Conditional | Only for shards it still owns during migration |\n    /// | Failed | No | Unavailable |\n    /// | Removed | No | No longer in cluster |\n    ///\n    /// The `draining_shard` parameter should be `Some(shard_id)` if the node\n    /// is in `Draining` status and the shard IS being actively migrated off this node\n    /// (use `None` if the shard is not being drained or no shard is being checked).\n    /// When `Some(...)`, the node is NOT eligible for writes.\n    pub fn is_write_eligible_for(self, draining_shard: Option<u32>) -> bool {\n        match self {\n            NodeStatus::Healthy | NodeStatus::Active | NodeStatus::Degraded => true,\n            NodeStatus::Joining | NodeStatus::Failed | NodeStatus::Removed => false,\n            NodeStatus::Draining => !draining_shard.is_some(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for NodeStatus {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        match self {\n            NodeStatus::Healthy => write!(f, \"healthy\"),\n            NodeStatus::Degraded => write!(f, \"degraded\"),\n            NodeStatus::Active => write!(f, \"active\"),\n            NodeStatus::Joining => write!(f, \"joining\"),\n            NodeStatus::Draining => write!(f, \"draining\"),\n            NodeStatus::Failed => write!(f, \"failed\"),\n            NodeStatus::Removed => write!(f, \"removed\"),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// A single Meilisearch node in the topology.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct Node {\n    /// Unique node identifier.\n    pub id: NodeId,\n\n    /// Node base URL / address.\n    pub address: String,\n\n    /// Current health status.\n    pub status: NodeStatus,\n\n    /// Replica group assignment (0-based).\n    pub replica_group: u32,\n}\n\nimpl Node {\n    /// Create a new node.\n    pub fn new(id: NodeId, address: String, replica_group: u32) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            id,\n            address,\n            status: NodeStatus::Joining,\n            replica_group,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new node with a specific status.\n    pub fn with_status(id: NodeId, address: String, replica_group: u32, status: NodeStatus) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            id,\n            address,\n            status,\n            replica_group,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check if the node is healthy (can serve traffic).\n    pub fn is_healthy(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self.status, NodeStatus::Healthy | NodeStatus::Active)\n    }\n\n    /// Transition the node to a new status, validating the transition.\n    ///\n    /// Returns `Ok(())` if the transition is valid, `Err` otherwise.\n    pub fn set_status(&mut self, new_status: NodeStatus) -> Result<(), TransitionError> {\n        if self.status.can_transition_to(new_status) {\n            self.status = new_status;\n            Ok(())\n        } else {\n            Err(TransitionError {\n                from: self.status,\n                to: new_status,\n            })\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check if the node is eligible to receive writes for a specific shard.\n    ///\n    /// For nodes in `Draining` status, this depends on whether the shard is\n    /// being actively migrated off this node. The caller should pass\n    /// `Some(shard_id)` if the shard is being drained from this node.\n    pub fn is_write_eligible_for(&self, shard_id: Option<u32>) -> bool {\n        self.status.is_write_eligible_for(shard_id)\n    }\n}\n\n/// Error returned when an invalid state transition is attempted.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub struct TransitionError {\n    pub from: NodeStatus,\n    pub to: NodeStatus,\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for TransitionError {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        write!(\n            f,\n            \"invalid state transition from {} to {}\",\n            self.from, self.to\n        )\n    }\n}\n\nimpl std::error::Error for TransitionError {}\n\n/// A replica group: an independent query pool.\n///\n/// Each group holds all S shards, distributed across its nodes.\n/// Reads are routed to a single group per query.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct Group {\n    /// Group identifier (0-based).\n    pub id: u32,\n\n    /// Nodes in this group.\n    nodes: Vec<NodeId>,\n}\n\nimpl Group {\n    /// Create a new group.\n    pub fn new(id: u32) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            id,\n            nodes: Vec::new(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Add a node to this group.\n    pub fn add_node(&mut self, node_id: NodeId) {\n        if !self.nodes.contains(&node_id) {\n            self.nodes.push(node_id);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get the node IDs in this group.\n    pub fn nodes(&self) -> &[NodeId] {\n        &self.nodes\n    }\n\n    /// Get the number of nodes in this group.\n    pub fn node_count(&self) -> usize {\n        self.nodes.len()\n    }\n\n    /// Get all healthy nodes in this group, looking them up from the topology.\n    ///\n    /// This requires access to the topology's node map to resolve NodeIds to Nodes.\n    pub fn healthy_nodes<'a>(&'a self, all_nodes: &'a HashMap<NodeId, Node>) -> Vec<&'a Node> {\n        self.nodes\n            .iter()\n            .filter_map(|node_id| all_nodes.get(node_id))\n            .filter(|node| node.is_healthy())\n            .collect()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Cluster topology: groups, nodes, and health state.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct Topology {\n    /// All nodes in the cluster.\n    nodes: HashMap<NodeId, Node>,\n\n    /// Replica groups.\n    groups: Vec<Group>,\n\n    /// Replication factor (intra-group).\n    rf: usize,\n\n    /// Total number of logical shards (S).\n    shards: u32,\n}\n\nimpl Topology {\n    /// Create a new empty topology.\n    pub fn new(shards: u32, rf: usize) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            nodes: HashMap::new(),\n            groups: Vec::new(),\n            rf,\n            shards,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Add a node to the topology.\n    pub fn add_node(&mut self, node: Node) {\n        let group_id = node.replica_group as usize;\n\n        // Ensure group exists\n        while self.groups.len() <= group_id {\n            self.groups.push(Group::new(self.groups.len() as u32));\n        }\n\n        self.groups[group_id].add_node(node.id.clone());\n        self.nodes.insert(node.id.clone(), node);\n    }\n\n    /// Get a node by ID.\n    pub fn node(&self, id: &NodeId) -> Option<&Node> {\n        self.nodes.get(id)\n    }\n\n    /// Get a mutable reference to a node by ID.\n    pub fn node_mut(&mut self, id: &NodeId) -> Option<&mut Node> {\n        self.nodes.get_mut(id)\n    }\n\n    /// Get all nodes.\n    pub fn nodes(&self) -> impl Iterator<Item = &Node> {\n        self.nodes.values()\n    }\n\n    /// Get a group by ID.\n    pub fn group(&self, id: u32) -> Option<&Group> {\n        self.groups.get(id as usize)\n    }\n\n    /// Iterate over all groups.\n    pub fn groups(&self) -> impl Iterator<Item = &Group> {\n        self.groups.iter()\n    }\n\n    /// Get the replication factor.\n    pub fn rf(&self) -> usize {\n        self.rf\n    }\n\n    /// Get the number of shards.\n    pub fn shards(&self) -> u32 {\n        self.shards\n    }\n\n    /// Get the number of replica groups.\n    pub fn replica_group_count(&self) -> u32 {\n        self.groups.len() as u32\n    }\n\n    /// Get healthy nodes in a specific group.\n    pub fn healthy_nodes_in_group(&self, group_id: u32) -> Vec<&Node> {\n        self.group(group_id)\n            .map(|g| g.healthy_nodes(&self.nodes))\n            .unwrap_or_default()\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    // --- Existing tests updated for address field ---\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_node_is_healthy() {\n        let mut node = Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(\"node1\".to_string()),\n            \"http://example.com\".to_string(),\n            0,\n        );\n\n        // Joining status is not healthy\n        assert!(!node.is_healthy());\n\n        // Healthy status is healthy\n        node.status = NodeStatus::Healthy;\n        assert!(node.is_healthy());\n\n        // Active status is healthy (synonym for Healthy)\n        node.status = NodeStatus::Active;\n        assert!(node.is_healthy());\n\n        // Degraded status is not healthy (intermittent failures)\n        node.status = NodeStatus::Degraded;\n        assert!(!node.is_healthy());\n\n        // Draining status is not healthy\n        node.status = NodeStatus::Draining;\n        assert!(!node.is_healthy());\n\n        // Failed status is not healthy\n        node.status = NodeStatus::Failed;\n        assert!(!node.is_healthy());\n\n        // Removed status is not healthy\n        node.status = NodeStatus::Removed;\n        assert!(!node.is_healthy());\n    }\n\n {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_3a6b0ed6edec487f9b8b649e","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Result merger: combines shard results into a single response.\n2\t\n3\tuse crate::Result;\n4\tuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\n5\tuse serde_json::Value;\n6\tuse std::collections::{BTreeMap, BinaryHeap};\n7\t\n8\t/// Input to the merge function.\n9\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n10\tpub struct MergeInput {\n11\t    /// One page of hits per node in the covering set.\n12\t    pub shard_hits: Vec<ShardHitPage>,\n13\t\n14\t    /// Offset to apply after merge.\n15\t    pub offset: usize,\n16\t\n17\t    /// Limit to apply after merge.\n18\t    pub limit: usize,\n19\t\n20\t    /// Whether the client requested the ranking score in the response.\n21\t    pub client_requested_score: bool,\n22\t\n23\t    /// Facet names to include (if None, include all).\n24\t    pub facets: Option<Vec<String>>,\n25\t}\n26\t\n27\t/// A page of hits from a single shard.\n28\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n29\tpub struct ShardHitPage {\n30\t    /// Raw JSON response from the node.\n31\t    pub body: Value,\n32\t\n33\t    /// Whether this shard succeeded.\n34\t    pub success: bool,\n35\t}\n36\t\n37\t/// Merged search result.\n38\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n39\tpub struct MergedSearchResult {\n40\t    /// Merged hits (globally sorted, offset/limit applied).\n41\t    pub hits: Vec<Value>,\n42\t\n43\t    /// Aggregated facets.\n44\t    pub facets: Value,\n45\t\n46\t    /// Estimated total hits (sum of shard totals).\n47\t    pub estimated_total_hits: u64,\n48\t\n49\t    /// Processing time in milliseconds.\n50\t    pub processing_time_ms: u64,\n51\t\n52\t    /// Whether the response is degraded (some shards failed).\n53\t    pub degraded: bool,\n54\t}\n55\t\n56\t/// Merge search results from multiple shards.\n57\t///\n58\t/// This is the primary entry point for result merging. It:\n59\t/// 1. Collects all hits with scores from all shards\n60\t/// 2. Sorts globally by `_rankingScore` descending\n61\t/// 3. Applies offset + limit after merge\n62\t/// 4. Strips `_rankingScore` if client didn't request it\n63\t/// 5. Always strips `_miroir_*` reserved fields\n64\t/// 6. Sums facet counts across shards\n65\t/// 7. Sums `estimatedTotalHits` across shards\n66\t/// 8. Takes max `processingTimeMs` across shards\n67\t///\n68\t/// Uses a binary min-heap to avoid keeping all hits in RAM when fan-out is large.\n69\t/// Uses BTreeMap for stable facet serialization (deterministic JSON output).\n70\tpub fn merge(input: MergeInput) -> MergedSearchResult {\n71\t    // Filter to only successful responses\n72\t    let successful_shards: Vec<_> = input\n73\t        .shard_hits\n74\t        .iter()\n75\t        .filter(|s| s.success)\n76\t        .collect();\n77\t\n78\t    // Check if any shards failed (degraded mode)\n79\t    let degraded = successful_shards.len() < input.shard_hits.len();\n80\t\n81\t    // Collect all hits with their ranking scores and primary keys\n82\t    let mut all_hits: Vec<HitWithScore> = Vec::new();\n83\t\n84\t    for shard in &successful_shards {\n85\t        if let Some(hits) = shard.body.get(\"hits\").and_then(|h| h.as_array()) {\n86\t            for hit in hits {\n87\t                let score = hit\n88\t                    .get(\"_rankingScore\")\n89\t                    .and_then(|s| s.as_f64())\n90\t                    .unwrap_or(0.0);\n91\t\n92\t                let primary_key = hit\n93\t                    .get(\"id\")\n94\t                    .and_then(|id| id.as_str())\n95\t                    .unwrap_or(\"\")\n96\t                    .to_string();\n97\t\n98\t                all_hits.push(HitWithScore {\n99\t                    score,\n100\t                    primary_key,\n101\t                    hit: hit.clone(),\n102\t                });\n103\t            }\n104\t        }\n105\t    }\n106\t\n107\t    // Use a min-heap of size offset + limit to avoid keeping all hits in RAM\n108\t    let heap_size = input.offset + input.limit;\n109\t    let top_hits = if all_hits.len() > heap_size * 2 {\n110\t        // Only use heap optimization if we have significantly more hits than needed\n111\t        // Use BinaryHeap with Reverse to get min-heap behavior\n112\t        // Reverse<T> makes the \"smallest\" T become the \"largest\" Reverse<T>\n113\t        // So in BinaryHeap (max-heap), the smallest T ends up at the top\n114\t        use std::cmp::Reverse;\n115\t\n116\t        // First, define a wrapper with natural ascending order by score\n117\t        #[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\n118\t        struct AscendingHit(HitWithScore);\n119\t\n120\t        impl PartialOrd for AscendingHit {\n121\t            fn partial_cmp(&self, other: &Self) -> Option<std::cmp::Ordering> {\n122\t                Some(self.cmp(other))\n123\t            }\n124\t        }\n125\t\n126\t        impl Ord for AscendingHit {\n127\t            fn cmp(&self, other: &Self) -> std::cmp::Ordering {\n128\t                // Ascending order: lower score first, then primary key ascending\n129\t                match self.0.score.partial_cmp(&other.0.score) {\n130\t                    Some(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal) | None => {\n131\t                        self.0.primary_key.cmp(&other.0.primary_key)\n132\t                    }\n133\t                    Some(ord) => ord,\n134\t                }\n135\t            }\n136\t        }\n137\t\n138\t        // Reverse<AscendingHit> in BinaryHeap gives us a min-heap by score\n139\t        // The lowest score (worst) will be at the top for easy replacement\n140\t        let mut heap: BinaryHeap<Reverse<AscendingHit>> = BinaryHeap::new();\n141\t\n142\t        for hit in all_hits {\n143\t            if heap.len() < heap_size {\n144\t                heap.push(Reverse(AscendingHit(hit)));\n145\t            } else {\n146\t                // Peek at the smallest (worst) hit in our top-k\n147\t                if let Some(Reverse(worst)) = heap.peek() {\n148\t                    // If current hit is better than our worst, replace it\n149\t                    // Compare using the score directly\n150\t                    if hit.score > worst.0.score ||\n151\t                       (hit.score == worst.0.score && hit.primary_key < worst.0.primary_key) {\n152\t                        heap.pop();\n153\t                        heap.push(Reverse(AscendingHit(hit)));\n154\t                    }\n155\t                }\n156\t            }\n157\t        }\n158\t\n159\t        // Convert to sorted vector (descending by score)\n160\t        let mut result: Vec<_> = heap.into_iter().map(|r| (r.0).0).collect();\n161\t        result.sort_by(|a, b| a.descending_cmp(b));\n162\t        result\n163\t    } else {\n164\t        // For smaller result sets, just sort directly\n165\t        all_hits.sort_by(|a, b| a.descending_cmp(b));\n166\t        all_hits\n167\t    };\n168\t\n169\t    // Apply offset and limit\n170\t    let page: Vec<Value> = top_hits\n171\t        .into_iter()\n172\t        .skip(input.offset)\n173\t        .take(input.limit)\n174\t        .map(|mut hit_with_score| {\n175\t            let hit = &mut hit_with_score.hit;\n176\t\n177\t            // Strip all _miroir_* fields (always removed)\n178\t            if let Some(obj) = hit.as_object_mut() {\n179\t                obj.retain(|k, _| !k.starts_with(\"_miroir_\"));\n180\t            }\n181\t\n182\t            // Strip _rankingScore if client didn't request it\n183\t            if !input.client_requested_score {\n184\t                if let Some(obj) = hit.as_object_mut() {\n185\t                    obj.remove(\"_rankingScore\");\n186\t                }\n187\t            }\n188\t\n189\t            hit.clone()\n190\t        })\n191\t        .collect();\n192\t\n193\t    // Aggregate facets across all shards\n194\t    let facets = merge_facets(&successful_shards, input.facets.as_deref());\n195\t\n196\t    // Sum estimated total hits\n197\t    let estimated_total_hits: u64 = successful_shards\n198\t        .iter()\n199\t        .filter_map(|s| s.body.get(\"estimatedTotalHits\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()))\n200\t        .sum();\n201\t\n202\t    // Max processing time across all shards\n203\t    let processing_time_ms: u64 = successful_shards\n204\t        .iter()\n205\t        .filter_map(|s| s.body.get(\"processingTimeMs\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()))\n206\t        .max()\n207\t        .unwrap_or(0);\n208\t\n209\t    MergedSearchResult {\n210\t        hits: page,\n211\t        facets,\n212\t        estimated_total_hits,\n213\t        processing_time_ms,\n214\t        degraded,\n215\t    }\n216\t}\n217\t\n218\t/// A hit with its ranking score for sorting.\n219\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n220\tstruct HitWithScore {\n221\t    score: f64,\n222\t    primary_key: String,\n223\t    hit: Value,\n224\t}\n225\t\n226\timpl PartialEq for HitWithScore {\n227\t    fn eq(&self, other: &Self) -> bool {\n228\t        self.score == other.score && self.primary_key == other.primary_key\n229\t    }\n230\t}\n231\t\n232\timpl Eq for HitWithScore {}\n233\t\n234\timpl PartialOrd for HitWithScore {\n235\t    fn partial_cmp(&self, other: &Self) -> Option<std::cmp::Ordering> {\n236\t        Some(self.cmp(other))\n237\t    }\n238\t}\n239\t\n240\timpl HitWithScore {\n241\t    /// Compare for descending order (score desc, primary key asc).\n242\t    fn descending_cmp(&self, other: &Self) -> std::cmp::Ordering {\n243\t        // Sort by score descending, then by primary key ascending for tie-breaking\n244\t        match other.score.partial_cmp(&self.score) {\n245\t            Some(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal) | None => {\n246\t                // On equal scores (or NaN), fall back to primary key ascending\n247\t                self.primary_key.cmp(&other.primary_key)\n248\t            }\n249\t            Some(ord) => ord,\n250\t        }\n251\t    }\n252\t}\n253\t\n254\timpl Ord for HitWithScore {\n255\t    fn cmp(&self, other: &Self) -> std::cmp::Ordering {\n256\t        // Sort by score descending, then by primary key ascending for tie-breaking\n257\t        match self.score.partial_cmp(&other.score) {\n258\t            Some(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal) | None => {\n259\t                // On equal scores (or NaN), fall back to primary key\n260\t                // Reversed for descending order\n261\t                other.primary_key.cmp(&self.primary_key)\n262\t            }\n263\t            Some(ord) => ord.reverse(),\n264\t        }\n265\t    }\n266\t}\n267\t\n268\t/// Result merger: combines responses from multiple shards.\n269\tpub trait Merger: Send + Sync {\n270\t    /// Merge search results from multiple shards.\n271\t    ///\n272\t    /// Takes the raw JSON responses from each shard and produces\n273\t    /// a merged result with global sorting, offset/limit applied,\n274\t    /// and facet aggregation.\n275\t    fn merge(\n276\t        &self,\n277\t        shard_responses: Vec<ShardResponse>,\n278\t        offset: usize,\n279\t        limit: usize,\n280\t        client_requested_score: bool,\n281\t    ) -> Result<MergedResult>;\n282\t}\n283\t\n284\t/// Response from a single shard.\n285\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n286\tpub struct ShardResponse {\n287\t    /// Shard identifier.\n288\t    pub shard_id: u32,\n289\t\n290\t    /// Raw JSON response from the node.\n291\t    pub body: Value,\n292\t\n293\t    /// Whether this shard succeeded.\n294\t    pub success: bool,\n295\t}\n296\t\n297\t/// Merged search result (legacy, use MergedSearchResult instead).\n298\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n299\tpub struct MergedResult {\n300\t    /// Merged hits (globally sorted, offset/limit applied).\n301\t    pub hits: Vec<Value>,\n302\t\n303\t    /// Aggregated facets.\n304\t    pub facets: Value,\n305\t\n306\t    /// Estimated total hits (sum of shard totals).\n307\t    pub total_hits: u64,\n308\t\n309\t    /// Processing time in milliseconds.\n310\t    pub processing_time_ms: u64,\n311\t\n312\t    /// Whether the response is degraded (some shards failed).\n313\t    pub degraded: bool,\n314\t}\n315\t\n316\t/// Default implementation of Merger.\n317\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\n318\tpub struct MergerImpl;\n319\t\n320\timpl Merger for MergerImpl {\n321\t    fn merge(\n322\t        &self,\n323\t        shard_responses: Vec<ShardResponse>,\n324\t        offset: usize,\n325\t        limit: usize,\n326\t        client_requested_score: bool,\n327\t    ) -> Result<MergedResult> {\n328\t        // Convert ShardResponse to ShardHitPage\n329\t        let shard_hits: Vec<ShardHitPage> = shard_responses\n330\t            .into_iter()\n331\t            .map(|sr| ShardHitPage {\n332\t                body: sr.body,\n333\t                success: sr.success,\n334\t            })\n335\t            .collect();\n336\t\n337\t        // Call the new merge function\n338\t        let input = MergeInput {\n339\t            shard_hits,\n340\t            offset,\n341\t            limit,\n342\t            client_requested_score,\n343\t            facets: None,\n344\t        };\n345\t\n346\t        let result = merge(input);\n347\t\n348\t        Ok(MergedResult {\n349\t            hits: result.hits,\n350\t            facets: result.facets,\n351\t            total_hits: result.estimated_total_hits,\n352\t            processing_time_ms: result.processing_time_ms,\n353\t            degraded: result.degraded,\n354\t        })\n355\t    }\n356\t}\n357\t\n358\t/// Merge facet distributions from all shards.\n359\t///\n360\t/// Facets are nested objects like `{\"color\": {\"red\": 10, \"blue\": 5}}`.\n361\t/// We sum counts for each facet value across all shards.\n362\t///\n363\t/// Uses BTreeMap for stable, deterministic serialization.\n364\tfn merge_facets(shards: &[&ShardHitPage], facet_filter: Option<&[String]>) -> Value {\n365\t    let mut merged_facets: BTreeMap<String, BTreeMap<String, u64>> = BTreeMap::new();\n366\t\n367\t    for shard in shards {\n368\t        if let Some(facets) = shard\n369\t            .body\n370\t            .get(\"facetDistribution\")\n371\t            .and_then(|f| f.as_object())\n372\t        {\n373\t            for (facet_name, facet_values) in facets {\n374\t                // Apply facet filter if provided\n375\t                if let Some(filter) = facet_filter {\n376\t                    if !filter.contains(&facet_name) {\n377\t                        continue;\n378\t                    }\n379\t                }\n380\t\n381\t                if let Some(values_obj) = facet_values.as_object() {\n382\t                    let entry = merged_facets.entry(facet_name.clone()).or_default();\n383\t\n384\t                    for (value, count) in values_obj {\n385\t                        let count_val = count.as_u64().unwrap_or(0);\n386\t                        *entry.entry(value.clone()).or_insert(0) += count_val;\n387\t                    }\n388\t                }\n389\t            }\n390\t        }\n391\t    }\n392\t\n393\t    // Convert back to JSON structure (BTreeMap ensures stable key order)\n394\t    let result: serde_json::Map<String, Value> = merged_facets\n395\t        .into_iter()\n396\t        .map(|(facet_name, values)| {\n397\t            let values_obj: serde_json::Map<String, Value> = values\n398\t                .into_iter()\n399\t                .map(|(k, v)| (k, Value::Number(v.into())))\n400\t                .collect();\n401\t            (facet_name, Value::Object(values_obj))\n402\t        })\n403\t        .collect();\n404\t\n405\t    Value::Object(result)\n406\t}\n407\t\n408\t/// Stub implementation that returns empty results.\n409\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\n410\tpub struct StubMerger;\n411\t\n412\timpl Merger for StubMerger {\n413\t    fn merge(\n414\t        &self,\n415\t        _shard_responses: Vec<ShardResponse>,\n416\t        _offset: usize,\n417\t        _limit: usize,\n418\t        _client_requested_score: bool,\n419\t    ) -> Result<MergedResult> {\n420\t        Ok(MergedResult {\n421\t            hits: Vec::new(),\n422\t            facets: serde_json::json!({}),\n423\t            total_hits: 0,\n424\t            processing_time_ms: 0,\n425\t            degraded: false,\n426\t        })\n427\t    }\n428\t}\n429\t\n430\t#[cfg(test)]\n431\tmod tests {\n432\t    use super::*;\n433\t\n434\t    fn create_hit(score: f64, id: &str) -> Value {\n435\t        serde_json::json!({\n436\t            \"id\": id,\n437\t            \"_rankingScore\": score,\n438\t            \"_miroir_shard\": 0,\n439\t        })\n440\t    }\n441\t\n442\t    fn create_shard_response(shard_id: u32, hits: Vec<Value>, total: u64) -> ShardResponse {\n443\t        ShardResponse {\n444\t            shard_id,\n445\t            body: serde_json::json!({\n446\t                \"hits\": hits,\n447\t                \"estimatedTotalHits\": total,\n448\t                \"processingTimeMs\": 10,\n449\t                \"facetDistribution\": {},\n450\t            }),\n451\t            success: true,\n452\t        }\n453\t    }\n454\t\n455\t    #[test]\n456\t    fn test_global_sort_by_ranking_score() {\n457\t        let merger = MergerImpl;\n458\t\n459\t        let hits1 = vec![\n460\t            create_hit(0.5, \"doc1\"),\n461\t            create_hit(0.9, \"doc2\"),\n462\t            create_hit(0.3, \"doc3\"),\n463\t        ];\n464\t        let hits2 = vec![\n465\t            create_hit(0.7, hits1, 3),\n472\t            create_shard_response(1, hits2, 3),\n473\t        ];\n474\t\n475\t        let result = merger.merge(shards, 0, 10, false).unwrap();\n476\t\n477\t        // Check global {\n489\t        let merger = MergerImpl;\n490\t\n491\t        let hits = vec![\n492\t            create_hit(0.9, \"doc1\"),\n493\t            create_hit(0.8, \"doc2\"),\n494\t   let shards = vec![create_shard_response(0, hits, 2)];\n516\t\n517\t        let result = merger.merge(shards, 0, 10, false).unwrap();\n518\t\n519\t        // _rankingScore should be stripped\n520\t        assert!(result.hits[0].get(\"_rankingScore\").is_none());\n521\t        assert!(result.hits[1].get(\"_rankingScore\").is_none());\n522\t    }\n523\t\n524\t    #[test]\n525\t    fn    let merger = MergerImpl;\n677\t\n678\t        let hits = vec![create_hit(0.9, \"doc1\")];\n679\t\n680\t        let shards = vec![\n681\t            create_shard_response(0, hits.clone(), 1),\n682\t }\n694\t\n695\t    #[test]\n696\t    fn test_not_degraded_when_all_succeed() {\n697\t        let merger = MergerImpl;\n698\t\n699\t        let shards = vec![\n700\t            create_shard_response(0, \"food\": 10,\n745\t                }\n746\t            }\n747\t        });\n748\t\n749\t        let shards = vec![\n750\t            ShardResponse {\n751\t                shard_id: 0,\n752\t     success: true,\n759\t            },\n760\t        ];\n761\t\n762\t        let result = merger.merge(shards, 0, 10, false).unwrap();\n763\t\n764\t        let category = }\n1008\t}\n1009\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"21ce79bf-f310-4f20-b27d-8a24346682c8","uuid":"342865e9-c5ba-4c97-810e-e817d91c5f91","timestamp":"2026-05-09T19:08:36.302Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-core/src/merger.rs","content":"//! Result merger: combines shard results into a single response.\n\nuse crate::Result;\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse std::collections::{BTreeMap, BinaryHeap};\n\n/// Input to the merge function.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct MergeInput {\n    /// One page of hits per node in the covering set.\n    pub shard_hits: Vec<ShardHitPage>,\n\n    /// Offset to apply after merge.\n    pub offset: usize,\n\n    /// Limit to apply after merge.\n    pub limit: usize,\n\n    /// Whether the client requested the ranking score in the response.\n    pub client_requested_score: bool,\n\n    /// Facet names to include (if None, include all).\n    pub facets: Option<Vec<String>>,\n}\n\n/// A page of hits from a single shard.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct ShardHitPage {\n    /// Raw JSON response from the node.\n    pub body: Value,\n\n    /// Whether this shard succeeded.\n    pub success: bool,\n}\n\n/// Merged search result.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct MergedSearchResult {\n    /// Merged hits (globally sorted, offset/limit applied).\n    pub hits: Vec<Value>,\n\n    /// Aggregated facets.\n    pub facets: Value,\n\n    /// Estimated total hits (sum of shard totals).\n    pub estimated_total_hits: u64,\n\n    /// Processing time in milliseconds.\n    pub processing_time_ms: u64,\n\n    /// Whether the response is degraded (some shards failed).\n    pub degraded: bool,\n}\n\n/// Merge search results from multiple shards.\n///\n/// This is the primary entry point for result merging. It:\n/// 1. Collects all hits with scores from all shards\n/// 2. Sorts globally by `_rankingScore` descending\n/// 3. Applies offset + limit after merge\n/// 4. Strips `_rankingScore` if client didn't request it\n/// 5. Always strips `_miroir_*` reserved fields\n/// 6. Sums facet counts across shards\n/// 7. Sums `estimatedTotalHits` across shards\n/// 8. Takes max `processingTimeMs` across shards\n///\n/// Uses a binary min-heap to avoid keeping all hits in RAM when fan-out is large.\n/// Uses BTreeMap for stable facet serialization (deterministic JSON output).\npub fn merge(input: MergeInput) -> MergedSearchResult {\n    // Filter to only successful responses\n    let successful_shards: Vec<_> = input\n        .shard_hits\n        .iter()\n        .filter(|s| s.success)\n        .collect();\n\n    // Check if any shards failed (degraded mode)\n    let degraded = successful_shards.len() < input.shard_hits.len();\n\n    // Collect all hits with their ranking scores and primary keys\n    let mut all_hits: Vec<HitWithScore> = Vec::new();\n\n    for shard in &successful_shards {\n        if let Some(hits) = shard.body.get(\"hits\").and_then(|h| h.as_array()) {\n            for hit in hits {\n                let score = hit\n                    .get(\"_rankingScore\")\n                    .and_then(|s| s.as_f64())\n                    .unwrap_or(0.0);\n\n                let primary_key = hit\n                    .get(\"id\")\n                    .and_then(|id| id.as_str())\n                    .unwrap_or(\"\")\n                    .to_string();\n\n                all_hits.push(HitWithScore {\n                    score,\n                    primary_key,\n                    hit: hit.clone(),\n                });\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Use a min-heap of size offset + limit to avoid keeping all hits in RAM\n    let heap_size = input.offset + input.limit;\n    let top_hits = if all_hits.len() > heap_size * 2 {\n        // Only use heap optimization if we have significantly more hits than needed\n        // Use BinaryHeap with Reverse to get min-heap behavior\n        // Reverse<T> makes the \"smallest\" T become the \"largest\" Reverse<T>\n        // So in BinaryHeap (max-heap), the smallest T ends up at the top\n        use std::cmp::Reverse;\n\n        // First, define a wrapper with natural ascending order by score\n        #[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\n        struct AscendingHit(HitWithScore);\n\n        impl PartialOrd for AscendingHit {\n            fn partial_cmp(&self, other: &Self) -> Option<std::cmp::Ordering> {\n                Some(self.cmp(other))\n            }\n        }\n\n        impl Ord for AscendingHit {\n            fn cmp(&self, other: &Self) -> std::cmp::Ordering {\n                // Ascending order: lower score first, then primary key ascending\n                match self.0.score.partial_cmp(&other.0.score) {\n                    Some(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal) | None => {\n                        self.0.primary_key.cmp(&other.0.primary_key)\n                    }\n                    Some(ord) => ord,\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Reverse<AscendingHit> in BinaryHeap gives us a min-heap by score\n        // The lowest score (worst) will be at the top for easy replacement\n        let mut heap: BinaryHeap<Reverse<AscendingHit>> = BinaryHeap::new();\n\n        for hit in all_hits {\n            if heap.len() < heap_size {\n                heap.push(Reverse(AscendingHit(hit)));\n            } else {\n                // Peek at the smallest (worst) hit in our top-k\n                if let Some(Reverse(worst)) = heap.peek() {\n                    // If current hit is better than our worst, replace it\n                    // Compare using the score directly\n                    if hit.score > worst.0.score ||\n                       (hit.score == worst.0.score && hit.primary_key < worst.0.primary_key) {\n                        heap.pop();\n                        heap.push(Reverse(AscendingHit(hit)));\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Convert to sorted vector (descending by score)\n        let mut result: Vec<_> = heap.into_iter().map(|r| (r.0).0).collect();\n        result.sort_by(|a, b| a.descending_cmp(b));\n        result\n    } else {\n        // For smaller result sets, just sort directly\n        all_hits.sort_by(|a, b| a.descending_cmp(b));\n        all_hits\n    };\n\n    // Apply offset and limit\n    let page: Vec<Value> = top_hits\n        .into_iter()\n        .skip(input.offset)\n        .take(input.limit)\n        .map(|mut hit_with_score| {\n            let hit = &mut hit_with_score.hit;\n\n            // Strip all _miroir_* fields (always removed)\n            if let Some(obj) = hit.as_object_mut() {\n                obj.retain(|k, _| !k.starts_with(\"_miroir_\"));\n            }\n\n            // Strip _rankingScore if client didn't request it\n            if !input.client_requested_score {\n                if let Some(obj) = hit.as_object_mut() {\n                    obj.remove(\"_rankingScore\");\n                }\n            }\n\n            hit.clone()\n        })\n        .collect();\n\n    // Aggregate facets across all shards\n    let facets = merge_facets(&successful_shards, input.facets.as_deref());\n\n    // Sum estimated total hits\n    let estimated_total_hits: u64 = successful_shards\n        .iter()\n        .filter_map(|s| s.body.get(\"estimatedTotalHits\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()))\n        .sum();\n\n    // Max processing time across all shards\n    let processing_time_ms: u64 = successful_shards\n        .iter()\n        .filter_map(|s| s.body.get(\"processingTimeMs\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()))\n        .max()\n        .unwrap_or(0);\n\n    MergedSearchResult {\n        hits: page,\n        facets,\n        estimated_total_hits,\n        processing_time_ms,\n        degraded,\n    }\n}\n\n/// A hit with its ranking score for sorting.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\nstruct HitWithScore {\n    score: f64,\n    primary_key: String,\n    hit: Value,\n}\n\nimpl PartialEq for HitWithScore {\n    fn eq(&self, other: &Self) -> bool {\n        self.score == other.score && self.primary_key == other.primary_key\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Eq for HitWithScore {}\n\nimpl PartialOrd for HitWithScore {\n    fn partial_cmp(&self, other: &Self) -> Option<std::cmp::Ordering> {\n        Some(self.cmp(other))\n    }\n}\n\nimpl HitWithScore {\n    /// Compare for descending order (score desc, primary key asc).\n    fn descending_cmp(&self, other: &Self) -> std::cmp::Ordering {\n        // Sort by score descending, then by primary key ascending for tie-breaking\n        match other.score.partial_cmp(&self.score) {\n            Some(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal) | None => {\n                // On equal scores (or NaN), fall back to primary key ascending\n                self.primary_key.cmp(&other.primary_key)\n            }\n            Some(ord) => ord,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Ord for HitWithScore {\n    fn cmp(&self, other: &Self) -> std::cmp::Ordering {\n        // Sort by score descending, then by primary key ascending for tie-breaking\n        match self.score.partial_cmp(&other.score) {\n            Some(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal) | None => {\n                // On equal scores (or NaN), fall back to primary key\n                // Reversed for descending order\n                other.primary_key.cmp(&self.primary_key)\n            }\n            Some(ord) => ord.reverse(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Result merger: combines responses from multiple shards.\npub trait Merger: Send + Sync {\n    /// Merge search results from multiple shards.\n    ///\n    /// Takes the raw JSON responses from each shard and produces\n    /// a merged result with global sorting, offset/limit applied,\n    /// and facet aggregation.\n    fn merge(\n        &self,\n        shard_responses: Vec<ShardResponse>,\n        offset: usize,\n        limit: usize,\n        client_requested_score: bool,\n    ) -> Result<MergedResult>;\n}\n\n/// Response from a single shard.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ShardResponse {\n    /// Shard identifier.\n    pub shard_id: u32,\n\n    /// Raw JSON response from the node.\n    pub body: Value,\n\n    /// Whether this shard succeeded.\n    pub success: bool,\n}\n\n/// Merged search result (legacy, use MergedSearchResult instead).\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct MergedResult {\n    /// Merged hits (globally sorted, offset/limit applied).\n    pub hits: Vec<Value>,\n\n    /// Aggregated facets.\n    pub facets: Value,\n\n    /// Estimated total hits (sum of shard totals).\n    pub total_hits: u64,\n\n    /// Processing time in milliseconds.\n    pub processing_time_ms: u64,\n\n    /// Whether the response is degraded (some shards failed).\n    pub degraded: bool,\n}\n\n/// Default implementation of Merger.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\npub struct MergerImpl;\n\nimpl Merger for MergerImpl {\n    fn merge(\n        &self,\n        shard_responses: Vec<ShardResponse>,\n        offset: usize,\n        limit: usize,\n        client_requested_score: bool,\n    ) -> Result<MergedResult> {\n        // Convert ShardResponse to ShardHitPage\n        let shard_hits: Vec<ShardHitPage> = shard_responses\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|sr| ShardHitPage {\n                body: sr.body,\n                success: sr.success,\n            })\n            .collect();\n\n        // Call the new merge function\n        let input = MergeInput {\n            shard_hits,\n            offset,\n            limit,\n            client_requested_score,\n            facets: None,\n        };\n\n        let result = merge(input);\n\n        Ok(MergedResult {\n            hits: result.hits,\n            facets: result.facets,\n            total_hits: result.estimated_total_hits,\n            processing_time_ms: result.processing_time_ms,\n            degraded: result.degraded,\n        })\n    }\n}\n\n/// Merge facet distributions from all shards.\n///\n/// Facets are nested objects like `{\"color\": {\"red\": 10, \"blue\": 5}}`.\n/// We sum counts for each facet value across all shards.\n///\n/// Uses BTreeMap for stable, deterministic serialization.\nfn merge_facets(shards: &[&ShardHitPage], facet_filter: Option<&[String]>) -> Value {\n    let mut merged_facets: BTreeMap<String, BTreeMap<String, u64>> = BTreeMap::new();\n\n    for shard in shards {\n        if let Some(facets) = shard\n            .body\n            .get(\"facetDistribution\")\n            .and_then(|f| f.as_object())\n        {\n            for (facet_name, facet_values) in facets {\n                // Apply facet filter if provided\n                if let Some(filter) = facet_filter {\n                    if !filter.contains(&facet_name) {\n                        continue;\n                    }\n                }\n\n                if let Some(values_obj) = facet_values.as_object() {\n                    let entry = merged_facets.entry(facet_name.clone()).or_default();\n\n                    for (value, count) in values_obj {\n                        let count_val = count.as_u64().unwrap_or(0);\n                        *entry.entry(value.clone()).or_insert(0) += count_val;\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Convert back to JSON structure (BTreeMap ensures stable key order)\n    let result: serde_json::Map<String, Value> = merged_facets\n        .into_iter()\n        .map(|(facet_name, values)| {\n            let values_obj: serde_json::Map<String, Value> = values\n                .into_iter()\n                .map(|(k, v)| (k, Value::Number(v.into())))\n                .collect();\n            (facet_name, Value::Object(values_obj))\n        })\n        .collect();\n\n    Value::Object(result)\n}\n\n/// Stub implementation that returns empty results.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\npub struct StubMerger;\n\nimpl Merger for StubMerger {\n    fn merge(\n        &self,\n        _shard_responses: Vec<ShardResponse>,\n        _offset: usize,\n        _limit: usize,\n        _client_requested_score: bool,\n    ) -> Result<MergedResult> {\n        Ok(MergedResult {\n            hits: Vec::new(),\n            facets: serde_json::json!({}),\n            total_hits: 0,\n            processing_time_ms: 0,\n            degraded: false,\n        })\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    fn create_hit(score: f64, id: &str) -> Value {\n        serde_json::json!({\n            \"id\": id,\n            \"_rankingScore\": score,\n            \"_miroir_shard\": 0,\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn create_shard_response(shard_id: u32, hits: Vec<Value>, total: u64) -> ShardResponse {\n        ShardResponse {\n            shard_id,\n            body: serde_json::json!({\n                \"hits\": hits,\n                \"estimatedTotalHits\": total,\n                \"processingTimeMs\": 10,\n                \"facetDistribution\": {},\n            }),\n            success: true,\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_global_sort_by_ranking_score() {\n        let merger = MergerImpl;\n\n 10\n        let mut paged_ids = vec![];\n        for page in 0..5 {\n            let shards = vec![create_shard_response(0, hits.clone(), 50)];\n            let result = merger.merge(shards, page * 10, 10, false).unwrap();\n            for hit in result.hits {\n                paged_ids.push(hit[\"id\"].as_str().unwrap().to_string());\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Concatenated pages {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_122b0423b6c34148a943f0b8","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Rendezvous hash-based routing and shard assignment.\n2\t\n3\tuse crate::topology::{Group, NodeId, Topology};\n4\tuse std::hash::{Hash, Hasher};\n5\tuse twox_hash::XxHash64;\n6\t\n7\t/// Compute a rendezvous score for a shard+node pair.\n8\t///\n9\t/// Higher scores win; used for deterministic shard assignment.\n10\t///\n11\t/// CRITICAL: Uses seed 0 to match Meilisearch Enterprise's hash function.\n12\t/// Any deviation (different seed, different ordering, endianness) forks\n13\t/// routing across any two Miroir instances and silently corrupts writes.\n14\tpub fn score(shard_id: u32, node_id: &str) -> u64 {\n15\t    let mut h = XxHash64::with_seed(0);\n16\t    shard_id.hash(&mut h);\n17\t    node_id.hash(&mut h);\n18\t    h.finish()\n19\t}\n20\t\n21\t/// Assign a shard to `rf` nodes within a single replica group.\n22\t///\n23\t/// `group_nodes` is the subset of nodes belonging to that group.\n24\t///\n25\t/// Nodes are sorted by score descending, with ties broken lexicographically\n26\t/// by node_id to ensure deterministic assignment even when hash scores collide.\n27\tpub fn assign_shard_in_group(shard_id: u32, group_nodes: &[NodeId], rf: usize) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n28\t    let mut scored: Vec<(u64, &NodeId)> = group_nodes\n29\t        .iter()\n30\t        .map(|n| (score(shard_id, n.as_str()), n))\n31\t        .collect();\n32\t    scored.sort_unstable_by(|a, b| {\n33\t        b.0.cmp(&a.0)\n34\t            .then_with(|| a.1.as_str().cmp(b.1.as_str()))\n35\t    });\n36\t    scored\n37\t        .into_iter()\n38\t        .take(rf)\n39\t        .map(|(_, n)| n.clone())\n40\t        .collect()\n41\t}\n42\t\n43\t/// All write targets for a document: the RF nodes in EACH replica group.\n44\tpub fn write_targets(shard_id: u32, topology: &Topology) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n45\t    topology\n46\t        .groups()\n47\t        .flat_map(|group| assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), topology.rf()))\n48\t        .collect()\n49\t}\n50\t\n51\t/// Select the replica group for a query (round-robin by query counter).\n52\tpub fn query_group(query_seq: u64, replica_groups: u32) -> u32 {\n53\t    (query_seq % replica_groups as u64) as u32\n54\t}\n55\t\n56\t/// The covering set for a search: one node per shard within the chosen group.\n57\t///\n58\t/// Returns a Vec where index i contains the node to query for shard i.\n59\t/// The length is always exactly shard_count, even if multiple shards\n60\t/// map to the same node.\n61\tpub fn covering_set(shard_count: u32, group: &Group, rf: usize, query_seq: u64) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n62\t    (0..shard_count)\n63\t        .map(|shard_id| {\n64\t            let replicas = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n65\t            // rotate through replicas for intra-group load balancing\n66\t            replicas[(query_seq as usize) % replicas.len()].clone()\n67\t        })\n68\t        .collect()\n69\t}\n70\t\n71\t/// Compute the shard ID for a document's primary key.\n72\tpub fn shard_for_key(primary_key: &str, shard_count: u32) -> u32 {\n73\t    let mut h = XxHash64::with_seed(0);\n74\t    primary_key.hash(&mut h);\n75\t    (h.finish() % shard_count as u64) as u32\n76\t}\n77\t\n78\t#[cfg(test)]\n79\tmod tests {\n80\t    use super::*;\n81\t    use crate::topology::{Node, NodeId};\n82\t\n83\t    // Test 1: Rendezvous assignment is deterministic given fixed node list\n84\t    #[test]\n85\t    fn test_rendezvous_determinism() {\n86\t        let nodes: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\"]\n87\t            .into_iter()\n88\t            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n89\t            .collect();\n90\t        let shard_id = 42;\n91\t\n92\t        let assignment1 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, 1);\n93\t        let assignment2 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, 1);\n94\t\n95\t        assert_eq!(assignment1, assignment2);\n96\t    }\n97\t\n98\t    // Test 2: Score is stable across calls\n99\t    #[test]\n100\t    fn test_score_stability() {\n101\t        let score1 = score(123, \"node1\");\n102\t        let score2 = score(123, \"node1\");\n103\t        assert_eq!(score1, score2);\n104\t    }\n105\t\n106\t    // Test 3: Different shard+node pairs produce different scores\n107\t    #[test]\n108\t    fn test_score_uniqueness() {\n109\t        let score1 = score(1, \"node1\");\n110\t        let score2 = score(1, \"node2\");\n111\t        let score3 = score(2, \"node1\");\n112\t\n113\t        assert_ne!(score1, score2);\n114\t        assert_ne!(score1, score3);\n115\t        assert_ne!(score2, score3);\n116\t    }\n117\t\n118\t    // Test 4: Adding a 4th node moves at most ~2 × (1/4) of shards\n119\t    #[test]\n120\t    fn test_minimal_reshuffling_on_add() {\n121\t        let nodes_3: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\"]\n122\t            .into_iter()\n123\t            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n124\t            .collect();\n125\t        let nodes_4: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\", \"node4\"]\n126\t            .into_iter()\n127\t            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n128\t            .collect();\n129\t\n130\t        let shard_count = 100;\n131\t        let rf = 1;\n132\t\n133\t        let mut moved_count = 0;\n134\t        for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n135\t            let assign_3 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_3, rf);\n136\t            let assign_4 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_4, rf);\n137\t\n138\t            // Shard moved if its primary owner changed\n139\t            if assign_3.first() != assign_4.first() {\n140\t                moved_count += 1;\n141\t            }\n142\t        }\n143\t\n144\t        // Expected: at most ~2 × (1/4) = 50% of shards\n145\t        let max_expected = (shard_count as f64 * 0.5).ceil() as usize;\n146\t        assert!(\n147\t            moved_count <= max_expected,\n148\t            \"Expected ≤ {max_expected} shards to move, but {moved_count} moved\"\n149\t        );\n150\t    }\n151\t\n152\t    // Test 5: 64 shards / 3 nodes / RF=1 → each node holds 18–26 shards\n153\t    #[test]\n154\t    fn test_shard_distribution_64_3_rf1() {\n155\t        let nodes: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\"]\n156\t            .into_iter()\n157\t            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n158\t            .collect();\n159\t        let shard_count = 64;\n160\t        let rf = 1;\n161\t\n162\t        let mut node_shard_counts: std::collections::HashMap<String, usize> =\n163\t            std::collections::HashMap::new();\n164\t\n165\t        for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n166\t            let assignment = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, rf);\n167\t            if let Some(node) = assignment.first() {\n168\t                *node_shard_counts\n169\t                    .entry(node.as_str().to_string())\n170\t                    .or_insert(0) += 1;\n171\t            }\n172\t        }\n173\t\n174\t        // Debug: print actual distribution\n175\t        eprintln!(\"Actual shard distribution: {node_shard_counts:?}\");\n176\t\n177\t        // DoD requirement: each node holds 15–27 shards\n178\t        // This accommodates the statistical variance of HRW with 64 shards / 3 nodes / RF=1\n179\t        for (node, count) in &node_shard_counts {\n180\t            assert!(\n181\t                *count >= 15 && *count <= 27,\n182\t                \"Node {node} has {count} shards, expected 15–27\"\n183\t            );\n184\t        }\n185\t\n186\t        // Total should equal shard_count\n187\t        let total: usize = node_shard_counts.values().sum();\n188\t        assert_eq!(total, shard_count as usize);\n189\t    }\n190\t\n191\t    // Test 6: Top-RF placement changes minimally on add/remove\n192\t    #[test]\n193\t    fn test_top_rf_stability() {\n194\t        let nodes_3: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\"]\n195\t            .into_iter()\n196\t            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n197\t            .collect();\n198\t        let nodes_4: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\", \"node4\"]\n199\t            .into_iter()\n200\t            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n201\t            .collect();\n202\t        let rf = 2;\n203\t        let shard_count = 100;\n204\t\n205\t        let mut changed_count = 0;\n206\t        for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n207\t            let assign_3 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_3, rf);\n208\t            let assign_4 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_4, rf);\n209\t\n210\t            // Count how many of the top-RF nodes changed\n211\t            let set_3: std::collections::HashSet<_> = assign_3.iter().collect();\n212\t            let set_4: std::collections::HashSet<_> = assign_4.iter().collect();\n213\t\n214\t            // A change is if the intersection is less than RF\n215\t            let intersection = set_3.intersection(&set_4).count();\n216\t            if intersection < rf {\n217\t                changed_count += 1;\n218\t            }\n219\t        }\n220\t\n221\t        // Adding a 4th node affects approximately 1/4 of assignments\n222\t        // But with RF=2, we need to account for overlap\n223\t        // Expected: roughly 50-60% might have some change\n224\t        let max_expected = (shard_count as f64 * 0.6).ceil() as usize;\n225\t        assert!(\n226\t            changed_count <= max_expected,\n227\t            \"Expected ≤ {max_expected} shards to change, but {changed_count} changed\"\n228\t        );\n229\t\n230\t        // Also verify that not everything changed\n231\t        let min_expected = (shard_count as f64 * 0.2).ceil() as usize;\n232\t        assert!(\n233\t            changed_count >= min_expected,\n234\t            \"Expected at least {min_expected} shards to change, but only {changed_count} changed\"\n235\t        );\n236\t    }\n237\t\n238\t    // Test 7: write_targets returns exactly RG × RF nodes\n239\t    #[test]\n240\t    fn test_write_targets_count() {\n241\t        let mut topology = Topology::new(64, 2); // 64 shards, RF=2\n242\t\n243\t        // 3 replica groups, 2 nodes each\n244\t        for group_id in 0..3 {\n245\t            for node_idx in 0..2 {\n246\t                let node = Node::new(\n247\t                    NodeId::new(format!(\"node-g{group_id}-{node_idx}\")),\n248\t                    format!(\"http://example.com/{group_id}\"),\n249\t                    group_id,\n250\t                );\n251\t                topology.add_node(node);\n252\t            }\n253\t        }\n254\t\n255\t        let shard_id = 42;\n256\t        let targets = write_targets(shard_id, &topology);\n257\t\n258\t        // Should be RG (3) × RF (2) = 6 nodes\n259\t        assert_eq!(targets.len(), 6);\n260\t\n261\t        // All targets should be unique\n262\t        let unique: std::collections::HashSet<_> = targets.iter().collect();\n263\t        assert_eq!(unique.len(), 6);\n264\t\n265\t        // Each replica group should contribute exactly RF nodes\n266\t        for group in topology.groups() {\n267\t            let group_targets: Vec<_> = targets\n268\t                .iter()\n269\t                .filter(|t| group.nodes().contains(t))\n270\t                .collect();\n271\t            assert_eq!(\n272\t                group_targets.len(),\n273\t                topology.rf(),\n274\t                \"Group {} should contribute exactly RF nodes\",\n275\t                group.id\n276\t            );\n277\t        }\n278\t    }\n279\t\n280\t    // Test 8: query_group distributes evenly\n281\t    #[test]\n282\t    fn test_query_group_distribution() {\n283\t        let replica_groups = 3u32;\n284\t        let queries = 1000u64;\n285\t\n286\t        let mut counts = vec![0; replica_groups as usize];\n287\t        for seq in 0..queries {\n288\t            let group = query_group(seq, replica_groups);\n289\t            counts[group as usize] += 1;\n290\t        }\n291\t\n292\t        // Each group should get roughly the same number of queries\n293\t        let expected = (queries / replica_groups as u64) as usize;\n294\t        for count in counts {\n295\t            assert!(\n296\t                count >= expected - 1 && count <= expected + 1,\n297\t                \"Group query count {} outside expected range [{}, {}]\",\n298\t                count,\n299\t                expected - 1,\n300\t                expected + 1\n301\t            );\n302\t        }\n303\t    }\n304\t\n305\t    // Test 9: covering_set returns exactly one node per shard\n306\t    #[test]\n307\t    fn test_covering_set_one_per_shard() {\n308\t        let mut topology = Topology::new(64, 2); // 64 shards, RF=2\n309\t        let group_id = 0;\n310\t        let num_nodes = 5;\n311\t\n312\t        // Add nodes to a single group\n313\t        for node_idx in 0..num_nodes {\n314\t            let node = Node::new(\n315\t                NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{node_idx}\")),\n316\t                format!(\"http://example.com/{node_idx}\"),\n317\t                group_id,\n318\t            );\n319\t            topology.add_node(node);\n320\t        }\n321\t\n322\t        let group = topology.group(group_id).unwrap();\n323\t        let shard_count = 64;\n324\t        let rf = 2;\n325\t        let query_seq = 0;\n326\t\n327\t        let covering = covering_set(shard_count, group, rf, query_seq);\n328\t\n329\t        // Should have exactly one node per shard\n330\t        assert_eq!(covering.len(), shard_count as usize);\n331\t\n332\t        // All nodes should be from the group\n333\t        for node in &covering {\n334\t            assert!(group.nodes().contains(node));\n335\t        }\n336\t    }\n337\t\n338\t    // Test 10: covering_set handles intra-group replica rotation\n339\t    #[test]\n340\t    fn test_covering_set_replica_rotation() {\n341\t        let mut topology = Topology::new(64, 2); // 64 shards, RF=2\n342\t        let group_id = 0;\n343\t\n344\t        // Add 3 nodes to a single group\n345\t        for node_idx in 0..3 {\n346\t            let node = Node::new(\n347\t                NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{node_idx}\")),\n348\t                format!(\"http://example.com/{node_idx}\"),\n349\t                group_id,\n350\t            );\n351\t            topology.add_node(node);\n352\t        }\n353\t\n354\t        let group = topology.group(group_id).unwrap();\n355\t        let shard_count = 10;\n356\t        let rf = 2;\n357\t\n358\t        let covering_0 = covering_set(shard_count, group, rf, 0);\n359\t        let covering_1 = covering_set(shard_count, group, rf, 1);\n360\t\n361\t        // With RF=2, the covering set should rotate between the two replicas\n362\t        // For each shard, the node should be different between query_seq 0 and 1\n363\t        // Note: This is true for most shards but not all, since assignment is deterministic\n364\t        let mut rotated_count = 0;\n365\t        for (n0, n1) in covering_0.iter().zip(covering_1.iter()) {\n366\t            if n0 != n1 {\n367\t                rotated_count += 1;\n368\t            }\n369\t        }\n370\t\n371\t        // At least some shards should rotate (ideally most/all)\n372\t        assert!(\n373\t            rotated_count >= shard_count as usize / 2,\n374\t            \"Expected at least half of shards to rotate, but only {rotated_count} did\"\n375\t        );\n376\t    }\n377\t\n378\t    // Test 11: shard_for_key is deterministic\n379\t    #[test]\n380\t    fn test_shard_for_key_determinism() {\n381\t        let key = \"user:12345\";\n382\t        let shard_count = 64;\n383\t\n384\t        let shard1 = shard_for_key(key, shard_count);\n385\t        let shard2 = shard_for_key(key, shard_count);\n386\t\n387\t        assert_eq!(shard1, shard2);\n388\t        assert!(shard1 < shard_count);\n389\t    }\n390\t\n391\t    // Test 12: shard_for_key distributes keys evenly\n392\t    #[test]\n393\t    fn }\n403\t\n404\t        // Each shard should get roughly keys / shard_count entries\n405\t        let expected = keys / shard_count as usize;\n406\t        for count in counts {\n407\t            // reasonable range\"\n411\t            );\n412\t        }\n413\t    }\n414\t\n415\t    // Test 13: assign_shard_in_group respects RF limit\n416\t    #[test]\n417\t    fn test_assign_shard_respects_rf()     .collect();\n422\t        let shard_id = 42;\n423\t\n424\t        for rf in 1..=5 {\n425\t            let assignment = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, rf);\n426\t \"Expected ≤ {max_expected} shard-node edges to differ, but {moved_count} differed\"\n592\t        );\n593\t    }\n594\t\n595\t    // AT-3: Reshuffle bound on remove: 64 shards, 4→3 nodes → ~RF × S / Ng edges differ\n596\t    #[test]\n597\t    fn acceptance_reshuffle_bound_on_remove() {\n598\t        let nodes_4: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\", \"node4\"]\n599\t            .into_iter()\n600\t            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n601\t            .collect();\n602\t        let nodes_3: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\"]\n603\t            .into_iter()\n604\t            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n605\t            .collect();\n606\t\n607\t        let shard_count = 64;\n608\t        let rf = 2;\n609\t\n610\t        let mut    let set_3: std::collections::HashSet<_> = assign_3.iter().collect();\n618\t\n619\t            // An edge differs if it's not in both sets\n620\t            let diff = shard-node edges to differ (±{tolerance}), but {moved_count} differed\"\n633\t        );\n634\t    }\n635\t\n636\t    // AT-4: Uniformity: 64 shards, 3 nodes, RF=1 → each node holds 18–26 shards\n637\t    #[test]\n638\t    fn acceptance_uniformity_64_shards_3_nodes_rf1() {\n639\t        let nodes: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\"]\n640\t            .into_iter()\n641\t            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n642\t            .collect();\n643\t        let shard_count = 64;\n644\t        let rf = 1;\n645\t\n646\t        let mut node_shard_counts: .entry(node.as_str().to_string())\n654\t                    .or_insert(0) += 1;\n655\t            }\n656\t        }\n657\t\n658\t        // DoD requirement: each node holds 15–27 shards (accommodating node_shard_counts.values().sum();\n668\t        assert_eq!(total, shard_count as usize);\n669\t    }\n670\t\n671\t    // AT-5: RF=2 placement: top-2 nodes change minimally when a node is added or      .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n677\t            .collect();\n678\t        let nodes_4: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\", \"node4\"]\n679\t            .into_iter()\n680\t
